examples.title: Algorithm: Preorder Traversal
examples.description: Preorder traversal visits the root node first, then the left subtree, and finally the right subtree.
examples.code:
ALGORITHM PreorderTraversal
INPUT: root node of the binary tree
OUTPUT: preorder traversal of the binary tree

BEGIN
 IF root is not null THEN
 visit root
 preorderTraversal(root's left child)
 preorderTraversal(root's right child)
 END IF
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the preorder traversal algorithm, which visits the root node first, then recursively traverses the left subtree, and finally the right subtree.

examples.title: Python: Preorder Traversal
examples.description: Implementation of preorder traversal in Python.
examples.code:
class Node:
 def __init__(self, value):
 self.value = value
 self.left = None
 self.right = None

def preorder_traversal(root):
 if root is not None:
 print(root.value)
 preorder_traversal(root.left)
 preorder_traversal(root.right)

# Example usage:
root = Node(1)
root.left = Node(2)
root.right = Node(3)
preorder_traversal(root)
examples.explanation: This Python code defines a Node class and a preorder_traversal function that recursively visits the root node, left subtree, and right subtree.

examples.title: C: Inorder Traversal
examples.description: Implementation of inorder traversal in C.
examples.explanation: This C code defines a Node struct and functions to create a new node and perform inorder traversal.
